title: Steady Beats Heroic
display_text: The ICW usually rewards steady planning more than heroics. A calm crew, a watchful eye, and a little patience often do more good than pushing too hard.
audio_text: The ICW usually rewards steady planning more than heroics. A calm crew and a little patience will often get you farther with less drama.
source_credit: Tip adapted from The Boat Galley / Carolyn Shearlock

title: Timing Matters
summary: A reminder that current and tide can shape an easy day or a difficult one.
display_text: A stop that looks simple on paper can become much less charming when current is running hard. Tide and current timing are often worth checking before the final approach.
audio_text: A stop that looks easy on paper can get lively when current is running. Tide and timing are often cheap insurance.
source_credit: Tip adapted from The Boat Galley / Carolyn Shearlock

title: Use Easy Provisioning
display_text: When a stop offers easy access to groceries, fuel, or other basics, it is usually wise to use it. Easy logistics are not something cruisers should take for granted.
audio_text: If a stop makes provisioning easy, take advantage of it. Easy logistics have a way of becoming harder tomorrow.
notes: General practical tip adapted for sidebar use.

title: Confirm the Approach
display_text: Before arrival, confirm the latest practical details you can: approach depth, current, and anything unusual about docking. Small checks often prevent big annoyances.
audio_text: A quick check on approach, current, and docking details before arrival can save a surprising amount of grief.
notes: General practical tip adapted for sidebar use.
